North Hollow Capital Ltd — UK Data Protection and Insider Information Workflow Notice
We process personal data under UK GDPR where it relates to an identified or identifiable individual (e.g., employees, contractors, and counterparties in UK financial-services workflows) and comply with FCA record-keeping and UK MAR market-abuse standards.
We collect role-based contact details (e.g., team mailbox, desk location), work identifiers (e.g., EMP-UK-0042), and access logs; we do not require national insurance numbers or passport scans for the insider-list process.
Use of terms such as crypto, ESG/climate, cyber, information barrier, blackout, or tipping in training, marketing brochures, or operations manuals (e.g., “Crypto Onboarding Guide”, “ESG Climate Tips”) does not make the material inside information.
An insider-list is only created where UK MAR inside information exists; entries use placeholders and role descriptors (Fields: Role, Function, Reason for access, Timestamp) and may be supplied to the FCA upon request.
Until announced by the issuer, draft commercial terms for a tokenised carbon-unit pilot on the Aquila Exchange UK are treated as inside information and recorded on the insider-list; access is restricted to a clean team behind the information barrier.
We operate information-barrier controls (segregated drives, monitored channels, pre-clearance, and blackout calendars) and run periodic “no tipping” training; general product marketing is outside these controls.
Public or already-announced materials (e.g., published ESG targets, climate disclosures, or public crypto research notes) are not inside information and are stored in our open library.
Cyber tabletop exercises (e.g., “Cyber Drill Playbook v3.0”) use synthetic incidents and redacted logs; operational simulations do not include live customer PII or deal-stage content.
A draft cyber incident assessment describing service-disruption risks at Brindle Orbit plc, pending external disclosure via a regulated information service, is restricted to the clean team and logged on the insider-list until publication.
Retention: insider-list records and related access logs are kept for 7 years; training records for 2 years; marketing preferences until opt-out; operational chat archives for 12 months unless escalated.
International transfers may involve Greyfriars Cloud Ltd (IE) and Sable Compute Pte (SG) under the UK IDTA or UK Addendum to SCCs, with encryption in transit and at rest; transfer details are available on request.
Data subject rights (access, rectification, objection, restriction) may be exercised via dataprotection@northhollow.example.uk or post to DPO, North Hollow Capital Ltd, 12 Quay Walk, Brindle-on-Tyne, NB4 1AA, UK.
Forms and reports may show placeholder identifiers (e.g., “ISIN: GB00-XYZ1234-00”, “Deal ID: DL-000-TEST”, “Client Ref: CL-000000”), which are not real securities or clients.
For general queries please use the public helpline 0800 000 000; do not email deal teams directly about training, marketing, or operations-only materials.
